research_metadata.important_limit: Historical JoSAA cutoffs for 2023-2025 are verified from the original user-provided dataset; CSAB 2025 Round 2 and Round 3 are backed by official PDFs. No historical value is labelled as an official 2026 cutoff.

college.full_official_name: Indian Institute of Information Technology (IIIT) Pune
college.ownership_type: Public-private partnership (PPP): Government of India, Government of Maharashtra and industry partners
college.governing_basis: Established under an Act of Parliament / IIIT PPP framework as described in the official JoSAA profile
college.full_campus_address: Gat No. 5 & 6, Village Nanoli-Tarf Chakan, P.O. Talegaon, Taluka Maval, District Pune, Maharashtra 410507

accreditation.note: Not verified; statutory-status implications require manual confirmation.
No institute-specific UGC record captured; deemed-university status is not claimed.
No verified numeric rank/listing captured from official rank and checked bands.

admission_routes.counselling_authority: JoSAA; CSAB Special Rounds where applicable
admission_routes.admission_process: Seat allocation through JoSAA and, for vacant seats, CSAB special rounds under applicable business rules
admission_routes.foreign_national_route: DASA route is referenced by the official profile; current DASA rules control
admission_routes.branch_change_rules: After the first two semesters; all common first-year credits in first attempt; minimum CGPA 8.5; merit by CGPA then JEE rank; subject to Senate decision and capacity

senior_junior_culture.summary: No reliable college-wide conclusion. A few student discussions imply peer help and coding interaction, but there are too few independent, identity-verifiable accounts to generalize about mentorship, hierarchy or ragging.
negative_themes: insufficient evidence on batch interaction and club hierarchy

academic_culture.summary: Official sources describe programmes and branch-change rules, but recent independent evidence on attendance strictness, grading, faculty accessibility and internship flexibility is insufficient.
positive_themes: official curriculum supports CSE/ECE and new specializations
branch change exists under a high-CGPA rule
negative_themes: student-level workload and flexibility not verified

hostel_reality.summary: The official 2026 profile describes permanent-campus hostel capacity, while some older negative reports concern the former transit-campus period. Recent public accounts are too limited to rate cleanliness, mess, water, curfew or complaint resolution.
positive_themes: new permanent-campus hostel buildings are publicly visible
negative_themes: current allotment guarantee, occupancy, mess fee and day-to-day service quality are not verified

club_and_campus_life.summary: Official material documents technical, sports, music, drama and entrepreneurship activities in 2022-24. Current 2026 activity of most clubs is not established from recent official posts.
negative_themes: many latest verifiable activities are older than one year
club_and_campus_life.evidence_strength: Moderate repeated signal for historical activity; limited for current activity

coding_and_placement_culture.summary: Official evidence shows coding competitions, hackathons and recognisable recruiters; recent student reports are generally positive about coding peers. Placement transparency is incomplete because counts, medians and role-wise results were not consistently published in accessible structured form.
negative_themes: placement-rate denominator and role/branch access are unclear
new specializations have no historical branch-specific outcomes

locality_and_student_life.summary: The permanent campus is in the Nanoli-Talegaon/Maval area rather than central Pune. Official distances support rail and road connectivity, but daily city access and late-evening transport convenience remain student-experience questions.
positive_themes: Talegaon railway station and bus stand are within the wider local area
regional industrial belt may offer hardware/manufacturing exposure
negative_themes: distance from central Pune and major technology clusters
public-transport frequency and last-mile options not verified

overall_satisfaction.summary: The available recent student evidence is too small and self-selected to estimate college-wide satisfaction or whether most students would choose the institute again.
positive_themes: new campus and peer coding are recurring positives
negative_themes: fees, placement transparency, location and unfinished-service uncertainty recur as decision questions

parent_mode_summary.label: perspective summary derived from collected evidence
parent_mode_summary.summary: IIIT Pune is a statutory Institute of National Importance with an expanded 674-seat B.Tech matrix for 2026. The last fee table captured totals ₹984,900 over four years in institutional charges, calculated from 2024-25 published year-wise values and excluding hostel/mess. Current hostel operating costs, a 2026-27 fee circular, final placement outcomes and median/role-wise reporting remain major verification needs. The permanent campus is near Talegaon rather than central Pune.
priority_points: Verify the 2026-27 fee circular and hostel/mess charges before budgeting.
Ask for final placement reports showing eligible, registered and placed students—not only average/highest CTC.
Confirm current hostel allotment, room occupancy, medical arrangements and last-mile transport.
Treat scholarships as potentially applicable until the latest notified-institution list and student-specific rules are checked.

student_mode_summary.label: perspective summary derived from collected evidence
student_mode_summary.summary: For 2026, IIIT Pune offers CSE, ECE and three newer specializations. The official record supports an active technical/coding environment historically, while recent student evidence is limited but generally positive about peer coding and the new campus. Students should weigh the campus's distance from central Pune, incomplete public evidence on hostel/mess operations and the lack of historical branch-specific placements/cutoffs for the newer specializations.
priority_points: Compare the curriculum—not just the branch label—for Cyber Security, AI & DS and Microelectronics/VLSI.
Ask whether specialized-branch students receive the same software/core placement eligibility as CSE/ECE.
Check active 2026 club recruitment rather than relying on older event listings.
Plan transport and internship logistics because the permanent campus is outside central Pune.
student_mode_summary.confidence_level: F — summary of official and student-reported evidence

decision_risks.detail: The available official profile embeds a 2024-25 fee table; a 2026-27 circular was not captured.
No current official hostel/mess fee or operating-service schedule was verified.
Official sources publish provisional 2025-batch on-campus counts and historical average/highest CTC, but median, role-wise, PPO and complete on/off-campus outcomes remain unavailable.
Cyber Security, AI & DS and Microelectronics/VLSI have no verified historical branch-specific cutoff or placement series in this package.
The official dynamic archive was checked but not exportable; 2025 and 2023 values are included only as low-confidence review aids, and 2024 is absent.
decision_risks.confidence_level: A for archive existence; E for rows

decision_risks.detail: The permanent campus is in the Nanoli-Talegaon area, not central Pune; daily city access may require planning.
Scheme eligibility depends on current notified lists, slots, category/income documents, renewal rules and portal deadlines.
Some reviews/maps refer to the former transit campus and must not be generalized to the permanent campus.

data_gaps: No concluded official 2026 cutoff series was available/verified for inclusion; historical values are not labelled as 2026 cutoffs.
CSAB special-round records for 2023 and 2024 are not included; add them only if three-year CSAB trend analysis is required.
Current 2026-27 fee circular, hostel fee, mess fee, deposits and category-wise waivers were not found.
Institute-specific AICTE ID/approval, NAAC grade/validity and NBA programme accreditation were not verified.
No verified NIRF engineering rank/score or institution-submitted NIRF data PDF was captured.

The official 2025-batch profile supplies provisional on-campus counts, percentages, internships and stipend extremes; final outcomes, medians, PPOs, roles and complete on/off-campus scope remain missing.
Recruiter roles, branch eligibility, selection counts and packages are mostly unpublished.
Current hostel occupancy/allotment guarantee, room-sharing, mess service, curfew and complaint-resolution details are not verified.
Exact coordinates, map-derived travel times, nearest practical hospital distance and city-centre distance require current map verification.
Recent official club pages/recruitment notices are sparse; most activity dates are historical.
Independent recent student evidence is insufficient for strong conclusions on academics, senior-junior culture, hostel quality and satisfaction.
